channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How Trump's mass layoffs raise the risk of wildfires in the US West, according to fired workers
item.description: The termination letters that ended the careers of thousands of U.S. Forest Service employees mean fewer people and less resources will be available to help prevent and fight wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle And Claire Rush, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge will consider further blocking Trump administration from freezing funds
item.description: A federal judge will on Friday consider a request to further block President Donald Trump ’s administration from freezing trillions of dollars of grants and loans that fund everything from clean energy programs to bridge repairs to emergency shelters. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Scramble for McConnell's Senate seat underway with signs of a bruising GOP primary ahead
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — The scramble to fill Mitch McConnell’s Senate seat in Kentucky began as soon as the long-serving Republican lawmaker revealed he won't seek reelection in 2026.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at $4B in funding for California high-speed rail
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Trump administration is once again targeting California's controversial high-speed rail project, with federal transportation officials on Thursday announcing an investigation and possible withdrawal of about $4 billion in federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CA gas prices highest in nation, Bay Area averaging $5-plus per gallon
item.description: (KRON) -- California gas prices are known to be significantly higher compared to other states in the country. On Thursday, the American Automobile Association (AAA) announced California gas is officially the priciest in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: IRS layoffs could hurt revenue collection and foil efforts to go after rich tax dodgers, experts say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The layoffs of roughly 7,000 IRS probationary workers beginning this week likely mean the end of the agency's plan to go after high-wealth tax dodgers and could spell disaster for revenue collections, experts say.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Climate change is shrinking glaciers faster than ever, with 7 trillion tons lost since 2000
item.description: A new and massive study finds that climate change is melting the world’s mountain glaciers faster than ever.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: McConnell won't seek reelection to Senate
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) announced Thursday that he is not seeking reelection in 2026, bringing an end to the longest-serving Senate leader’s political career.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump Education pick Linda McMahon clears committee, heads to full Senate
item.description: Linda McMahon, President Trump’s pick for Education secretary, passed the Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions (HELP) Committee on Thursday morning, sending her confirmation to the full Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Most worried DOGE cuts could hurt services in their communities: Survey
item.description: Most U.S. adults in the latest survey, 62 percent, disagreed with the notion that the president is authorized to terminate federal employees who do not align with his views or agenda.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Social justice law professor defends anti-Israel protesters who blocked traffic to Chicago airport
item.description: A Northwestern Law School clinic is offering free legal defense to a group of anti-Israel activists who shut down traffic to Chicago's O'Hare International Airport last spring.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Navy veteran's service dogs denied access on plane in Denver
item.description: A Navy veteran missed his flight after not being allowed to take his service dogs on a plane from Denver to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Shaul Turner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month into second term: Survey
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating appears to be underwater as many suggest he may be overstepping his authority, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump backs idea to send 20% of DOGE savings to American citizens
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said that he likes the idea of giving some of the savings from Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency back to U.S. citizens as a kind of dividend.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: After watching videos of a Delta Air Lines jet catch fire upon landing and flip over on a Toronto runway, it's fair to wonder how anyone could have survived.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Arkansas victims of mortuary theft scheme inspire push for law banning sale of human remains
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A year had passed since Doneysha Smith suffered a stillbirth and the memories of her loss lingered in 2023.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New, cheaper iPhone 16e has 'best battery life ever,' Apple says
item.description: "iPhone 16e has the best battery life ever on a 6.1-inch iPhone, lasting up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE," Apple said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Colorado city helps dozens of families relocate after Tren de Aragua apartment infestation
item.description: The Aurora, Colorado police chief said Wednesday that efforts to clean up an apartment complex infested with Venezuelan gang members, involved relocating 85 families.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This rookie QB card could be worth $50K if you pull it from a new Topps set
item.description: Denver Broncos rookie quarterback Bo Nix impressed on the field, and now a Colorado sports card store is ready to pay $50,000 to keep a unique card featuring him close to home.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KRON4

item.title: Department of Homeland Security fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Former NFL player arrested while protesting MAGA at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a Huntington Beach City Council meeting on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the town's Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Musk says he will check with Trump about DOGE stimulus checks
item.description: (KRON) -- Elon Musk said he will check with President Donald Trump about the possibility of sending $8,000 "DOGE stimulus" checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: The white man who pleaded guilty to shooting a Black teen who rang a wrong doorbell dies
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — An 86-year-old Missouri man has died just days after pleading guilty to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, prosecutors announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds rally against firing of federal workers, emphasizing threats to public health
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hundreds of current and former federal employees rallied Wednesday outside the Department of Health and Human Services headquarters, protesting recent firings and their effect on public health, and directing much of their ire at Elon Musk and his agency's efforts to cut spending.
item.dc:creator: NATHAN ELLGREN and ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cynthia Erivo to host 78th Annual Tony Awards
item.description: "Wicked" actress Cynthia Erivo is set to host the 78th Annual Tony Awards. “I am so proud and excited to take on this glorious honor,” said Erivo.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Polar vortex makes much of US colder than Greenland, but warmth is coming. Then more cold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The polar vortex hit its peak across much of America on Wednesday, with an icy grip that made Arctic Greenland seem like a toasty vacation spot in comparison. Even Mars has been warmer than North Dakota this week. But there's hope.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KRON4

item.title: Trump's firing of 1,000 national park workers raises concerns about maintenance and operating hours
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has fired about 1,000 newly hired National Park Service employees who maintain and clean parks, educate visitors and perform other functions as part of its broad-based effort to downsize government.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspects charged in connection to burglaries of several pro athletes
item.description: Seven Chilean nationals face federal charges related to the burglaries of several high profile athletes' homes.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: Kamala Harris signs with major Hollywood talent agency
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris has signed with the Creative Artists Agency, one of Hollywood's top talent and sports agencies. In a post on Instagram, CAA revealed signed with the agency "for representation in all areas, with a focus on speaking engagements and publishing.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signs order to expand White House control of independent agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aiming to expand the White House’s authority over various independent regulatory agencies, a move likely to prompt legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
